summary: Matched and cleaned up several item-related functions, especially Mario Cape and Maxim Tomato behavior, while moving item-specific prototypes out of the generic it_27CF header into dedicated headers. The PR also improved struct typing and bitfield access for common item vars, replaced raw M2C/gm pointer arithmetic with named fields, and added a compile flag to suppress bitfield constant conversion warnings. Evidence for author intent is limited because the PR body/review text is empty, but the diff shows decomp-matching cleanup, header hygiene, and small type/signature fixes across fighter item-spawn call sites.
what_changed: Added an implementation/prototype for Mario Cape item spawn function it_802B2560 in itmariocape.c/.h, constructing a SpawnItem, setting parent gobj fields, facing direction, damage, zero velocity, b0 spawn flag, and attaching the item to the fighter part with Item_8026AB54 plus db_80225DD8.

Added Mario Cape pickup and animation behavior: it_2725_Logic41_PickedUp resets item command vars, selects animation based on ftLib_800865CC, calls Item_802694CC and it_80274574, and itMariocape_UnkMotion1_Anim handles async effects and cape removal/reset checks through ftMr_SpecialS_CheckItemCapeRemove and ftMr_SpecialS_Reset.
Moved Mario Cape and Link Bomb prototypes from generic it_27CF.h toward item-specific headers, adding includes for itmariocape.h in ftMr_SpecialS.c and itlinkbomb.h in ftLk_SpecialLw.c.
Changed Link Bomb spawn signature it_8029DD58 and its helper argument from u8 to u32 in both source and header.

Refactored Maxim Tomato item vars in itCommonItems.h from a nested flags union to direct bitfield bool x4_b0 plus documented offsets, and renamed special attribute fields to heal_amount_0 and heal_amount_1.

Updated ittomato.c to use Fighter_GObj* parent_gobj naming, direct typed MaximTomatoSpecialAttr access, direct tomato.x4_b0 bitfield access, gm_80473A18.x90 array access on destroy, JOBJ_HIDDEN instead of raw 0x10, and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE | ITEM_DROP_UPDATE instead of raw flag value 6.
Simplified itheart destroy logic from manual sign/bit manipulation over flags and raw gm pointer arithmetic to ip->xDD4_itemVar.heart.xDD8.b0 and gm_80473A18.x90[index] access.
Improved lbArq_80014ABC signature from s32 lbArq_80014ABC(UNK_T) to u32 lbArq_80014ABC(lbArqNode*) and replaced M2C_FIELD(arg0, s32*, 4) with arg0->state.
Adjusted item common structs for Thunder vars and attributes: x60/x64 became s32, and ThunderPokemonAttributes x4/x8 became f32.
Added -Wno-bitfield-constant-conversion to compile_flags.txt, likely to accommodate signed/boolean bitfield definitions introduced or exposed by this cleanup.
Performed minor formatting cleanup in Samus bomb header, Sheik chain spawn line wrapping, and removed an extra blank line in Samus special-lw source.
smart_moves: Localizing prototypes to item-specific headers reduced reliance on the broad it_27CF.h catch-all header and made fighter call sites include the actual item module they use.
Replacing M2C_FIELD and raw pointer/bit manipulation with named struct fields made the decompiled logic more readable without changing the apparent behavior.
Using existing named constants such as JOBJ_HIDDEN, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, and ITEM_DROP_UPDATE preserved semantic intent and avoids magic numbers in future matching work.
The Mario Cape implementation uses a small helper for effect spawning and a reset inline to keep the matched function structure manageable while still exposing high-level behavior.
Changing lbArq_80014ABC to take lbArqNode* and return arg0->state demonstrates a useful pattern: once a struct is understood, replace UNK_T and offset loads with typed field access.
The Tomato and Heart destroy paths now share the same decomp style: check a named item-var bitfield and clear gm_80473A18.x90[index], which makes similar item lifecycle cleanup easier to recognize.
decomp_lessons: When a generic item header accumulates unrelated prototypes, move declarations into the owning item header and update direct users to include that header. This improves dependency clarity and can prevent prototype drift.

If a bitfield represents a sign-sensitive single-bit test, the declared signedness can matter for matching; this PR changed itHeart_ItemVars.xDD8.b0 from u8 to s8 and added a warning suppression for bitfield constant conversion.

Replace M2C_FIELD only after the backing struct layout is understood; the Tomato special attributes and lbArq node state conversions are good examples of turning offset-based code into typed field access.
Prefer project constants for HSD/JObj/item update flags once identified. Raw values such as 0x10 or 6 obscure intent and make later audits harder.
Use consistent local names like ip for Item*, gobj for Item_GObj*, parent_gobj for Fighter_GObj*, and child for HSD_JObj* child pointers to match common melee decomp style and reduce ambiguity.

SpawnItem initialization patterns should set both prev_pos and pos, parent gobj fields, facing_dir, zero damage/velocity, x44_flag.b0, and x40 before calling Item_80268B18 when matching item-spawn helpers.
naming_conventions: Item* locals are standardized toward ip rather than item or it in changed functions.
Item_GObj* return/local variables are named gobj instead of item_gobj where the surrounding function already takes a parent_gobj.
Fighter-spawning item helpers use parent_gobj for the owning Fighter_GObj* argument.
MaximTomatoSpecialAttr fields were renamed from x0/x4-style unknowns to heal_amount_0 and heal_amount_1 where semantics were inferred.
itTomato_ItemVars uses a named bool bitfield x4_b0 instead of a nested x4.b0 flag union.
JObj child pointers may be named child instead of jobj2 when the role is known.
lbArq_80014ABC now names its argument lbArqNode* arg0 rather than UNK_T, with direct state field access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced PAD_STACK(4) and PAD_STACK(8) in Mario Cape functions, indicating stack padding was needed to preserve matching.

Used inline helper functions in itmariocape.c, including it_8029DD58_inline-style existing pattern for Link Bomb and new inline/reset helpers for Mario Cape, to shape generated code while keeping C readable.
Changed bitfield declarations and added -Wno-bitfield-constant-conversion, suggesting bitfield signedness/constant assignment was part of the matching or compiler-cleanliness strategy.
Converted a complex signed bit extraction in itheart.c into a signed bitfield read, preserving the likely generated condition while greatly improving source readability.
Moved function prototypes to specific headers to make call signatures exact at call sites, including u32 widening for it_8029DD58 and proper Fighter_GObj*/Item_GObj* distinctions.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body or human text excerpt was available, so reviewer intent and rationale are inferred only from the diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename it_802B2560 or Mario Cape functions after itmariocape.c matching.
Search for remaining prototypes in it_27CF.h that belong in item-specific headers.
Audit other item destroy handlers for raw gm_80473A18 pointer arithmetic that can become gm_80473A18.x90[index].
Search for remaining M2C_FIELD access to x4_specialAttributes in item code and replace with typed special attribute structs where layouts are known.
Check whether compile flag -Wno-bitfield-constant-conversion remains necessary after future bitfield cleanups.
